text: Advances in optical sensor technology produce larger images. With more and more electronic devices that are capable of recording UHD (ultra-high definition) video and photos, the processing capacity required in the image processing workflow has significantly increased. One of the core image processing tasks in such workflows is Low-Light Image Enhancement (LLIE). Here, we release a 4K and 8K dataset (LISED) with 7000 pairs of low and enhanced images and then do experimental evaluations to several existing methods. On this footing, we develop a lightweight transformer-based method called LLFormer. Different from existing works that mostly utilize vertical attention, in our approach, we introduce the axis-based multi-head self-attention (AMS) for both spatial feature extraction and channel attention to increase processing throughput to less than linear complexity. Additionally, to further enhance performance, a cross-layer attention fusion block is introduced at the output layer. The experimental comparisons on our LISED dataset and other benchmarking public Low-light datasets demonstrate the superior performance of our approach. Moreover, we explore the impact of using the our benchmark and other publicly available datasets to pretrain the existing popular LLIE methods and the experimental results for a simple yet commonly used downstream task such as face detection further confirm that our pretraining scheme enhances significantly the overall performance. Our source code and the pre-trained models are released at https://github.com/TaoWangzj/LLFormer.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
